   150→
   151→// ============================================
   152→// Sales Pipeline Service
   153→// ============================================
   154→
   155→export class SalesPipelineService {
   156→  // ----------------------------------------
   157→  // OPPORTUNITIES
   158→  // ----------------------------------------
   159→
   160→  static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
   161→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   162→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   163→      .select(`
   164→        *,
   165→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   166→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   167→      `)
   168→      .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES)
   169→      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   170→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   171→
   172→    if (error) {
   173→      console.error('Error fetching sales pipeline opportunities:', error);
   174→      throw error;
   175→    }
   176→    return (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[];
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  static async create(data: CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   180→    // Dedup check: name + tg_handle
   181→    if (data.name && data.tg_handle) {
   182→      const dedupKey = `${data.name.toLowerCase().trim()}::${data.tg_handle.toLowerCase().trim()}`;
   183→      const { data: existing } = await supabase
   184→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   185→        .select('id')
   186→        .eq('dedup_key', dedupKey)
   187→        .maybeSingle();
   188→
   189→      if (existing) {
   190→        throw new Error('A deal with this name and TG handle already exists');
   191→      }
   192→
   193→      (data as any).dedup_key = dedupKey;
   194→    }
   195→
   196→    const insertData = {
   197→      ...data,
   198→      stage: data.stage || 'cold_dm',
   199→    };
   200→
   201→    const { data: created, error } = await supabase
   202→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   203→      .insert([insertData])
   204→      .select()
   205→      .single();
   206→
   207→    if (error) throw error;
   208→
   209→    // Record stage history
   210→    await CRMService.recordStageHistory('opportunity', created.id, null, insertData.stage || 'cold_dm');
   211→
   212→    return created as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   213→  }
   214→
   215→  static async update(
   216→    id: string,
   217→    updates: Partial<CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData> & {
   218→      warm_sub_state?: WarmSubState | null;
   219→      orbit_reason?: OrbitReason | null;
   220→      closed_lost_reason?: string | null;
   221→      next_meeting_at?: string | null;
   222→      next_meeting_type?: string | null;
   223→      bump_number?: number;
   224→      last_bump_date?: string | null;
   225→      bucket?: Bucket | null;
   226→      calendly_sent_via?: CalendlySentVia;
   227→      calendly_sent_date?: string | null;
   228→      calendly_booked_date?: string | null;
   229→      gc_opened?: string;
   230→    }
   231→  ): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   232→    // Get current state for stage history
   233→    const { data: current } = await supabase
   234→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   235→      .select('*')
   236→      .eq('id', id)
   237→      .single();
   238→
   239→    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
```

   289→  static async recordBump(id: string): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {

   368→  const handleStageChange = async (oppId: string, newStage: SalesPipelineStage, currentStage: string) => {
   369→    if (newStage === 'orbit') {
   370→      setOrbitPrompt({ oppId, fromStage: currentStage });
   371→      return;
   372→    }
   373→    if (newStage === 'v2_closed_lost') {
   374→      setClosedLostPrompt({ oppId, fromStage: currentStage });
   375→      return;
   376→    }
   377→    try {
   378→      await SalesPipelineService.update(oppId, { stage: newStage as OpportunityStage });
   379→      await fetchData();
   380→    } catch (err) {
   381→      console.error('Error changing stage:', err);
   382→    }
   383→  };
   384→
   385→  const confirmOrbit = async () => {
   386→    if (!orbitPrompt) return;
   387→    try {
   388→      await SalesPipelineService.update(orbitPrompt.oppId, {
   389→        stage: 'orbit' as OpportunityStage,
   390→        orbit_reason: orbitReasonValue,
   391→      });
   392→      setOrbitPrompt(null);
   393→      setOrbitReasonValue('no_response');
   394→      await fetchData();
   395→    } catch (err) {

   407→      setClosedLostPrompt(null);

   438→        await fetchActivities(oppId);
   439→      }
   440→    } catch (err) {
   441→      console.error('Error recording bump:', err);
   442→      await fetchData(); // revert on error
   443→    } finally {
   444→      setIsBumping(false);
   445→    }
   446→  };
   447→
   448→  const handleReduceBump = async (oppId: string) => {
   449→    if (isBumping) return;
   450→    setIsBumping(true);
   451→    // Optimistic update
   452→    setOpportunities(prev => prev.map(o =>
   453→      o.id === oppId ? { ...o, bump_number: Math.max(o.bump_number - 1, 0) } : o
   454→    ));
   455→    try {
   456→      await SalesPipelineService.reduceBump(oppId);
   457→      await fetchData();
   458→    } catch (err) {
   459→      console.error('Error reducing bump:', err);
```

   574→    const totalItems = tabbedKOLs.length;

   576→    const startIndex = (currentPage - 1) * ITEMS_PER_PAGE;
   577→    const endIndex = startIndex + ITEMS_PER_PAGE;
   578→    const paginatedKOLs = tabbedKOLs.slice(startIndex, endIndex);
   579→
   580→    return {
   581→      totalItems,
   582→      totalPages,
   583→      startIndex,
   584→      endIndex,
   585→      paginatedKOLs,
   586→      currentPage,
   587→    };
   588→  }, [tabbedKOLs, currentPage, ITEMS_PER_PAGE]);

   594→
   595→  const handleCellSave = async () => {
   596→    if (!editingCell) return;

   598→      const kolToUpdate = kols.find(k => k.id === editingCell.kolId);
   599→      if (!kolToUpdate) return;
   600→
   601→      // Check for duplicate link
   602→      if (editingCell.field === 'link' && editingValue && editingValue.trim()) {
   603→        const duplicateKOL = kols.find(k =>
   604→          k.id !== editingCell.kolId &&
   605→          k.link &&
   606→          k.link.trim().toLowerCase() === editingValue.trim().toLowerCase()
   607→        );
   608→
   609→        if (duplicateKOL) {
   610→          toast({
   611→            title: 'Duplicate Link',
   612→            description: `This link is already used by "${duplicateKOL.name || 'another KOL'}"`,
   613→            variant: 'destructive',
   614→            duration: 5000,
   615→          });
   616→          setEditingCell(null);
   617→          setEditingValue(null);
   618→          return;
   619→        }

   483→  const handleInlineEdit = async (oppId: string, field: string, value: string) => {
   484→    try {
   485→      const updates: any = {};
   486→      if (field === 'deal_value') {
   487→        updates.deal_value = value ? parseFloat(value) : null;
   488→      } else if (field === 'temperature_score') {
   489→        updates.temperature_score = parseInt(value) || 50;
   490→      } else {
   491→        updates[field] = value || null;
   492→      }
   493→      await SalesPipelineService.update(oppId, updates);
   494→      await fetchData();
   495→    } catch (err) {
   496→      console.error('Error inline edit:', err);
   497→    }
   498→    setEditingCell(null);
   499→  };
   500→
   501→  // ============================================
   502→  // DnD Handlers
   503→  // ============================================
   504→
   505→  const handleDragStart = (event: DragStartEvent) => {
   506→    const id = event.active.id as string;
   507→    setActiveId(id);
   508→    setActiveOpportunity(opportunities.find(o => o.id === id) || null);
   509→  };
   510→
   511→  const handleDragEnd = async (event: DragEndEvent) => {
   512→    const { active, over } = event;
   513→    setActiveId(null);
   514→    setActiveOpportunity(null);
   515→
   516→    if (!over) return;
   517→
   518→    const oppId = active.id as string;
   519→    const overId = over.id as string;
   520→    const opp = opportunities.find(o => o.id === oppId);
   521→    if (!opp) return;
   522→
   523→    // Check if dropped on a stage column
   524→    const isStageColumn = ALL_V2_STAGES.includes(overId as SalesPipelineStage);
   525→
   526→    if (isStageColumn) {
   527→      const newStage = overId as SalesPipelineStage;
   528→      if (opp.stage !== newStage) {
   529→        await handleStageChange(oppId, newStage, opp.stage);
   530→      }
   531→    } else {
   532→      // Dropped on another card — reorder within same stage
   533→      const targetOpp = opportunities.find(o => o.id === overId);
   534→      if (targetOpp && targetOpp.stage === opp.stage) {
   535→        const stageOpps = getStageOpps(opp.stage as SalesPipelineStage);
   536→        const oldIndex = stageOpps.findIndex(o => o.id === oppId);
   537→        const newIndex = stageOpps.findIndex(o => o.id === overId);
   538→        if (oldIndex !== -1 && newIndex !== -1) {
   539→          const reordered = arrayMove(stageOpps, oldIndex, newIndex);
   540→          const positions = reordered.map((o, i) => ({ id: o.id, position: i }));
   541→          await SalesPipelineService.updatePositions(positions);
   542→          await fetchData();
   543→        }
   544→      } else if (targetOpp && targetOpp.stage !== opp.stage) {
   545→        // Dropped on card in different stage
   546→        await handleStageChange(oppId, targetOpp.stage as SalesPipelineStage, opp.stage);
   547→      }
   548→    }
   549→  };
   550→
   551→  // ============================================
   552→  // Open slide-over
   553→  // ============================================

   555→  const openSlideOver = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
   556→    setSlideOverOpp(opp);
   557→    setSlideOverMode('view');
   558→    fetchActivities(opp.id);
   559→    setActivityForm({ opportunity_id: opp.id, type: 'note', title: '' });

   573→      deal_value: opp.deal_value || undefined,
   574→      currency: opp.currency,
   575→      notes: opp.notes || undefined,
   576→    });
   577→  };

   449→  // ----------------------------------------
   450→  // POSITION UPDATES
   451→  // ----------------------------------------
   452→
   453→  static async updatePositions(positions: { id: string; position: number; stage?: OpportunityStage }[]): Promise<void> {
   454→    const updates = positions.map(({ id, position, stage }) => {
   455→      const updateData: any = { position, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   456→      if (stage) updateData.stage = stage;
   457→      return supabase
   458→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   459→        .update(updateData)
   460→        .eq('id', id);
   461→    });
   462→
   463→    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   464→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   465→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   466→      console.error('Errors updating positions:', errors);
   467→      throw errors[0].error;
   468→    }
   469→  }
   470→}
   471→
```

   564→  // Bulk update handler
   565→  const handleBulkUpdate = async () => {
   566→    if (selectedOpportunities.length === 0 || Object.keys(bulkEdit).length === 0) return;
   567→
   568→    // Filter out undefined values
   569→    const updates: Record<string, any> = {};
   570→    Object.entries(bulkEdit).forEach(([key, value]) => {
   571→      if (value !== undefined) {
   572→        updates[key] = value;
   573→      }
   574→    });
   575→
   576→    if (Object.keys(updates).length === 0) return;
   577→
   578→    try {
   579→      // Optimistic update
   580→      setOpportunities(prev =>
   581→        prev.map(o => selectedOpportunities.includes(o.id) ? { ...o, ...updates } : o)
   582→      );
   583→
   584→      // Update in database
   585→      await Promise.all(
   586→        selectedOpportunities.map(id => CRMService.updateOpportunity(id, updates))
   587→      );
   588→
   589→      // Clear selection and bulk edit
   590→      setSelectedOpportunities([]);
   591→      setBulkEdit({});
   592→    } catch (error) {
   593→      console.error('Error bulk updating:', error);
   594→    }
   595→  };

   599→    setEditingValue(currentValue?.toString() || '');
   600→  };

   602→  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent, oppId: string, field: string) => {
   603→    if (e.key === 'Enter') {
   604→      e.preventDefault();
   605→      const value = field === 'deal_value' ? (parseFloat(editingValue) || null) : editingValue;
   606→      handleInlineUpdate(oppId, field, value);
   607→    } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   608→      setEditingCell(null);
   609→      setEditingValue('');
   610→    }
   611→  };

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-02-24 04:26:57Z)**_

I now have a thorough understanding of all the patterns. Let me compile the complete findings.

---

Here is a comprehensive report of every pattern you need to know before building the Outreach tab.

---

## 1. Current Sales Pipeline Page Structure

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`

### Tab Architecture (lines 2223-2283)

The page uses shadcn `<Tabs>` with `value={activeTab}` controlled by `useState<'pipeline' | 'orbit'>('pipeline')`. There are exactly two tabs today:

```tsx
<Tabs value={activeTab} onValueChange={v => setActiveTab(v as 'pipeline' | 'orbit')}>
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="pipeline">Pipeline</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="orbit">
      Orbit
      {orbitOpps.length > 0 && <Badge variant="secondary">{orbitOpps.length}</Badge>}
    </TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>

  {/* Per-tab controls rendered conditionally inline */}
  {activeTab === 'pipeline' && <Select pathFilter ... />}
  {activeTab === 'pipeline' && <ViewModeToggle kanban/table />}

  <TabsContent value="pipeline" className="mt-0">
    {viewMode === 'kanban' ? renderKanban() : renderTable()}
  </TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="orbit" className="mt-0">
    {renderOrbitTab()}
  </TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

The tab's type union (`'pipeline' | 'orbit'`) needs to become a 3-way union to add `'outreach'`. The tab-specific controls (path filter, view mode toggle) are rendered `{activeTab === 'pipeline' && ...}` inline between `<TabsList>` and `<TabsContent>`.

### Pipeline Tab — renderTable() (lines 885-1097)

Structure: one collapsible section per `visiblePipelineStages` (array of `SalesPipelineStage`). Each section is:
- A styled header div with stage label, count Badge, and total value
- A `<Table>` with `DndContext` wrapping all sections
- Rows are `<SortableTableRow>` which wraps a `TableRow` with DnD handles
- The **cold_dm** stage gets one extra column: `Bumps` (bump_number out of 4, rendered as dots)
- Per-row dropdown menu with "Record Bump" action only available when `stage === 'cold_dm'`

The `searchTerm` and `pathFilter` filter is applied before rendering via `filteredOpportunities`, a simple `.filter()` on the entire in-memory array (line 304-309).

### Orbit Tab — renderOrbitTab() (lines 1098-1217)

A flat list of collapsible groups, one per `OrbitReason` value. Each group has its own `<Table>` with columns: Name, Bucket, DM, Temp, Value, Owner, Time in Orbit, Last Contacted, actions. No DnD, no inline edit. Row click opens the same slide-over.

### Filtering Pattern

Currently only two filters exist for the entire page:
- `searchTerm` — simple substring match on `opp.name`
- `pathFilter` — `'all' | 'closer' | 'sdr'` → filters by `dm_account`

Both are applied client-side in `filteredOpportunities` (lines 304-309). There is no server-side filtering at all today.

---

## 2. SalesPipelineService — lib/salesPipelineService.ts

### getAll() (line 160-177)

Fetches **all** records in a single Supabase call with no pagination or filtering:

```ts
static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
  const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from('crm_opportunities')
    .select(`*, affiliate:crm_affiliates(*), client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)`)
    .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES)        // filters to v2 stages only
    .order('position', { ascending: true })
    .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
  return (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[];
}
```

**No `.range()` exists anywhere in this service.** The service has no server-side pagination support at all. You will need to add a new paginated method.

### Available Filter Fields on crm_opportunities (confirmed via live schema query)

Full column list for filter building:

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `stage` | text | Primary filter — use `.eq()` or `.in()` |
| `dm_account` | text | `'closer'`, `'sdr'`, `'other'` |
| `bucket` | text | `'A'`, `'B'`, `'C'`, null |
| `bump_number` | integer | 0-4 — great for "needs bump" filter |
| `last_bump_date` | timestamptz | null = never bumped |
| `tg_handle` | text | null = no handle yet |
| `owner_id` | uuid | assign filter |
| `source` | text | lead source |
| `temperature_score` | integer | default 50 |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | date range filter |
| `last_contacted_at` | timestamptz | staleness filter |
| `gc_opened` | text | default `'na'` |
| `warm_sub_state` | text | `'interested'`, `'silent'`, null |
| `dedup_key` | text | null = not deduped yet |
| `name` | text | full-text search |

### Bump Mechanics (lines 289-343)

`recordBump(id)`:
1. Reads current `bump_number`
2. Increments by 1
3. Sets `last_bump_date` and `last_contacted_at` to now
4. Creates a `crm_activities` row of type `'bump'` with title `"Bump #N"`

`reduceBump(id)`:
1. Decrements `bump_number` by 1 (min 0)
2. No activity log

Cold DM opps in the Pipeline table show bumps as a `{bump_number}/4` count with 4 dots. The per-row dropdown shows "Record Bump" only when `stage === 'cold_dm'`. The bump handler (`handleRecordBump`) does an **optimistic update** — it immediately updates local state before the async call, then re-fetches on completion.

### updatePositions() (lines 453-469)

Takes an array of `{ id, position, stage? }`, issues parallel `supabase.update()` calls for each. Used by the DnD reorder handler.

---

## 3. Existing Pagination Patterns

**Key finding:** There is a `components/ui/pagination.tsx` component, but it is **not used anywhere in the app**. All actual pagination is hand-rolled with `Button` + `useState(currentPage)`.

### The `components/ui/pagination.tsx` Component

A complete shadcn pagination component exposing:
- `<Pagination>` — outer `<nav>`
- `<PaginationContent>` — `<ul>` flex row
- `<PaginationItem>` — `<li>` wrapper
- `<PaginationLink isActive?>` — renders as `<a>`, uses `buttonVariants`
- `<PaginationPrevious>` / `<PaginationNext>` — labeled arrow links
- `<PaginationEllipsis>` — `...` spacer

**This component is unused.** Every paginated page uses custom Button-based controls.

### The KOL Page Pattern — Most Sophisticated (app/kols/page.tsx)

This is the best reference for the Outreach tab's pagination. It uses **client-side slicing**, not server-side `.range()`:

```ts
// State
const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState(1);
const ITEMS_PER_PAGE = 50;

// Memoized pagination (lines 572-588)
const paginationData = useMemo(() => {
  const totalItems = tabbedKOLs.length;
  const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalItems / ITEMS_PER_PAGE);
  const startIndex = (currentPage - 1) * ITEMS_PER_PAGE;
  const endIndex = startIndex + ITEMS_PER_PAGE;
  const paginatedKOLs = tabbedKOLs.slice(startIndex, endIndex);
  return { totalItems, totalPages, startIndex, endIndex, paginatedKOLs, currentPage };
}, [tabbedKOLs, currentPage, ITEMS_PER_PAGE]);
```

**Pagination UI (lines 3107-3161):**
```tsx
{paginationData.totalPages > 1 && (
  <div className="flex items-center justify-between mt-4 pb-14 px-4">
    <div className="text-sm text-gray-600">
      Showing {paginationData.startIndex + 1}-{Math.min(paginationData.endIndex, paginationData.totalItems)} of {paginationData.totalItems} KOLs
    </div>
    <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
      <Button variant="outline" size="sm"
        onClick={() => setCurrentPage(prev => Math.max(1, prev - 1))}
        disabled={currentPage === 1}>
        <ChevronLeft className="h-4 w-4" /> Previous
      </Button>
      {/* Sliding window of 5 page numbers */}
      {Array.from({ length: Math.min(5, paginationData.totalPages) }, (_, i) => {
        let pageNum: number;
        if (paginationData.totalPages <= 5) pageNum = i + 1;
        else if (currentPage <= 3) pageNum = i + 1;
        else if (currentPage >= paginationData.totalPages - 2) pageNum = paginationData.totalPages - 4 + i;
        else pageNum = currentPage - 2 + i;
        return (
          <Button key={pageNum} variant={currentPage === pageNum ? 'default' : 'outline'} size="sm"
            onClick={() => setCurrentPage(pageNum)}
            className="w-8 h-8 p-0"
            style={currentPage === pageNum ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' } : {}}>
            {pageNum}
          </Button>
        );
      })}
      <Button variant="outline" size="sm"
        onClick={() => setCurrentPage(prev => Math.min(paginationData.totalPages, prev + 1))}
        disabled={currentPage === paginationData.totalPages}>
        Next <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4" />
      </Button>
    </div>
  </div>
)}
```

### Simpler Pattern (campaigns, sops, lists pages)

All three use the same minimal pattern:
```tsx
const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState(1);
const itemsPerPage = 12; // or 10
const totalPages = Math.ceil(filteredItems.length / itemsPerPage);
const pagedItems = filteredItems.slice((currentPage - 1) * itemsPerPage, currentPage * itemsPerPage);
// UI: Previous / "Page X of Y" text / Next buttons only — no numbered page buttons
```

### Server-Side .range() — Does Not Exist Yet

There is **zero usage of `.range()` anywhere** in the codebase. If you want true server-side pagination (required for 1000-2000 cold DMs), you'll need to write a new service method. The pattern would be:

```ts
static async getColdDmsPaginated(
  page: number,
  pageSize: number,
  filters: { dm_account?: DmAccount; bucket?: Bucket; searchTerm?: string }
): Promise<{ data: SalesPipelineOpportunity[]; count: number }> {
  const from = (page - 1) * pageSize;
  const to = from + pageSize - 1;

  let query = supabase
    .from('crm_opportunities')
    .select(`*, affiliate:crm_affiliates(*), client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)`, { count: 'exact' })
    .eq('stage', 'cold_dm')
    .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
    .range(from, to);

  if (filters.dm_account) query = query.eq('dm_account', filters.dm_account);
  if (filters.bucket) query = query.eq('bucket', filters.bucket);
  if (filters.searchTerm) query = query.ilike('name', `%${filters.searchTerm}%`);

  const { data, error, count } = await query;
  if (error) throw error;
  return { data: (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[], count: count || 0 };
}
```

The `{ count: 'exact' }` option on `.select()` returns the total row count alongside the data, which you need for `totalPages = Math.ceil(count / pageSize)`.

---

## 4. Bulk Action Patterns

### Best Reference: app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx

This is the **only place in the app** with row-level checkbox selection + bulk operations on a table. Key patterns:

**State:**
```ts
const [selectedOpportunities, setSelectedOpportunities] = useState<string[]>([]);
const [bulkEdit, setBulkEdit] = useState<Partial<CRMOpportunity>>({});
```

**Row checkbox (lines 1440-1473) — hover-to-reveal pattern:**
```tsx
<TableCell className="text-gray-500 text-sm w-10">
  {(() => {
    const isChecked = selectedOpportunities.includes(opp.id);
    return (
      <div className="flex items-center justify-center">
        {isChecked ? (
          <Checkbox checked={true} onCheckedChange={(checked) => {
            if (checked) setSelectedOpportunities(prev => [...prev, opp.id]);
            else setSelectedOpportunities(prev => prev.filter(id => id !== opp.id));
          }} />
        ) : (
          <>
            <span className="block group-hover:hidden">{index + 1}</span>  {/* row number when not hovered */}
            <span className="hidden group-hover:flex">
              <Checkbox checked={false} onCheckedChange={(checked) => {
                if (checked) setSelectedOpportunities(prev => [...prev, opp.id]);
              }} />
            </span>
          </>
        )}
      </div>
    );
  })()}
</TableCell>
```

This requires `className="group hover:bg-gray-50"` on the `<TableRow>`.

**Bulk action toolbar (lines 2229-2435) — conditional card above the table:**
```tsx
{selectedOpportunities.length > 0 && (
  <div className="bg-white border border-gray-200 rounded-lg p-6 shadow-sm">
    <div className="flex items-center gap-3 mb-4">
      <span>{selectedOpportunities.length} opportunities selected</span>
      <Button onClick={selectAll/deselectAll}>Select All / Deselect All</Button>
    </div>
    <div className="flex flex-wrap items-end gap-3">
      {/* Dropdowns for Stage, Owner, Source, Affiliate, Client, Scope */}
      <Button onClick={handleBulkUpdate} disabled={Object.keys(bulkEdit).length === 0}>Apply Changes</Button>
      <Button onClick={() => setIsBulkDeleteDialogOpen(true)} className="bg-red-600">Delete</Button>
      <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => { setSelectedOpportunities([]); setBulkEdit({}); }}>Cancel</Button>
    </div>
  </div>
)}
```

**Bulk update handler (lines 565-594):**
```ts
const handleBulkUpdate = async () => {
  // Optimistic update
  setOpportunities(prev =>
    prev.map(o => selectedOpportunities.includes(o.id) ? { ...o, ...updates } : o)
  );
  // Parallel DB updates
  await Promise.all(selectedOpportunities.map(id => CRMService.updateOpportunity(id, updates)));
  setSelectedOpportunities([]);
  setBulkEdit({});
};
```

### Secondary Reference: campaigns/[id]/page.tsx

Has `handleBulkDeletePayments`, `handleBulkDeleteContents`, `handleBulkStatusChange` — all operate on selected IDs stored in state, but these are for content/payment rows not CRM rows, and don't use checkboxes on table rows in the same way.

---

## 5. cold_dm Stage Handling Summary

- All `cold_dm` opps appear in the Pipeline tab's `renderTable()` as the first section (position 0 in `PIPELINE_STAGES`)
- The "Cold DM" section gets an **extra Bumps column** only when `stage === 'cold_dm'` (conditional `<TableHead>` at line 930 and `<TableCell>` at line 1021-1032)
- The row action dropdown shows "Record Bump" only for `cold_dm` rows (line 1053-1057)
- `handleRecordBump` does **optimistic update** first, then calls `SalesPipelineService.recordBump()`
- Dashboard metric `dashboardMetrics.responseRate` treats any opp that has advanced past `cold_dm` as a "response" (line 199-200)
- `handleResurrect` (from Orbit) moves opps back to `cold_dm` and clears `orbit_reason`
- The dedup check in `create()` is based on `name + tg_handle` — relevant if you're bulk importing cold DMs

---

## Key Architecture Decisions for the Outreach Tab

Based on all patterns above, here is what to reuse and what to build new:

**Reuse exactly as-is:**
- `Tabs` / `TabsList` / `TabsTrigger` / `TabsContent` pattern — just add a third value `'outreach'` to the union
- The tab trigger with a Badge showing count (like Orbit does)
- The Checkbox `components/ui/checkbox` — it's already imported in other pages
- The hover-to-reveal checkbox + row number pattern from `crm/pipeline/page.tsx` lines 1440-1473
- The bulk toolbar pattern from `crm/pipeline/page.tsx` lines 2229-2435 (conditional card, Apply/Delete/Cancel buttons)
- The `useMemo` pagination data object pattern from `app/kols/page.tsx` lines 572-588
- The 5-page sliding window pagination UI from `app/kols/page.tsx` lines 3107-3161 (with `backgroundColor: '#3e8692'` for the active page)
- `SalesPipelineService.recordBump()` and the optimistic update pattern in `handleRecordBump`
- The `STAGE_COLORS`, `BUCKET_COLORS`, `STAGE_LABELS` constants

**Build new:**
- `SalesPipelineService.getColdDmsPaginated(page, pageSize, filters)` — new method using `.select(..., { count: 'exact' })` + `.range(from, to)` + chained `.eq()` / `.ilike()` filters
- Separate page state for the Outreach tab: `outreachPage`, `outreachPageSize` (suggested 50-100), `outreachTotalCount`, `outreachFilters`
- A `useEffect` that re-fetches server-side when `outreachPage`, `outreachFilters`, or `activeTab === 'outreach'` changes
- Bulk bump action (new to this tab): bulk-select N cold_dm rows → "Bump All" calls `recordBump()` for each selected ID
- Bulk stage-advance action: "Move to Warm" calls `SalesPipelineService.update(id, { stage: 'warm' })` for each selected ID
- Bulk delete action (same pattern as `crm/pipeline/page.tsx` bulk delete)
- Filter controls specific to the Outreach tab: `dm_account` select, `bucket` select, bump count range, date added range — rendered only when `activeTab === 'outreach'`

```md
# Outreach Tab — Scale Cold DMs to 1000-2000

## Context

The sales pipeline page currently loads ALL opportunities (including cold DMs) in a single `getAll()` call and renders them in the Pipeline tab. At 1000-2000 cold DMs this breaks: slow load, unusable kanban column, no bulk actions. Cold DMs are a high-volume outreach workflow that needs its own dedicated tab with server-side pagination, filters, and bulk operations.

**Approach:** Add an "Outreach" tab between Pipeline and Orbit. Pipeline tab stops showing `cold_dm` stage. Outreach tab gets its own server-side paginated query, filters, checkbox multi-select, and bulk actions.

---

## Step 1: Service — New paginated method in `lib/salesPipelineService.ts`

Add `getColdDmsPaginated()`:

\```ts
static async getColdDmsPaginated(
  page: number,
  pageSize: number,
  filters: {
    dm_account?: DmAccount;
    bucket?: Bucket;
    bumpRange?: 'none' | '1-2' | '3+';
    searchTerm?: string;
    sortBy?: string;
    sortAsc?: boolean;
  }
): Promise<{ data: SalesPipelineOpportunity[]; count: number }>
\```

- Uses `.select(..., { count: 'exact' })` + `.eq('stage', 'cold_dm')` + `.range(from, to)`
- Chains optional `.eq('dm_account', ...)`, `.eq('bucket', ...)`, `.ilike('name', ...)` filters
- Bump range filter: `none` → `.eq('bump_number', 0)`, `1-2` → `.gte(1).lte(2)`, `3+` → `.gte(3)`
- Sort by `created_at` (default), `last_bump_date`, `bump_number`, `name`, `temperature_score`

Also add `bulkRecordBump(ids: string[])` — calls `recordBump()` for each ID in parallel.

Also add `bulkUpdateStage(ids: string[], stage: SalesPipelineStage)` — updates stage for multiple opps.

---

## Step 2: Remove `cold_dm` from Pipeline tab

In `page.tsx`, update the Pipeline tab's visible stages to exclude `cold_dm`:

- Filter `visiblePipelineStages` to skip `cold_dm` (both kanban columns and table sections)
- Pipeline tab now starts at `warm` stage
- Existing `getAll()` continues to fetch all v2 stages (used by Pipeline + Orbit + dashboard metrics)

---

## Step 3: Add Outreach tab to `page.tsx`

### New state variables

\```ts
const [outreachOpps, setOutreachOpps] = useState<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]>([]);
const [outreachTotal, setOutreachTotal] = useState(0);
const [outreachPage, setOutreachPage] = useState(1);
const [outreachLoading, setOutreachLoading] = useState(false);
const [outreachFilters, setOutreachFilters] = useState({ dm_account: undefined, bucket: undefined, bumpRange: undefined, searchTerm: '' });
const [selectedOutreach, setSelectedOutreach] = useState<string[]>([]);
const [isBulkBumping, setIsBulkBumping] = useState(false);
\```

### Data fetching

New `fetchOutreach()` function that calls `getColdDmsPaginated()`. Triggered by:
- `useEffect` when `outreachPage`, `outreachFilters`, or `activeTab === 'outreach'` changes
- After bulk actions complete

### Tab structure

Update `activeTab` type to `'outreach' | 'pipeline' | 'orbit'`. Add new TabsTrigger with `MessageSquare` icon and count badge showing `outreachTotal`. Tab order: Outreach → Pipeline → Orbit (matches funnel flow).

### Tab-specific controls (rendered when `activeTab === 'outreach'`)

- Search input (debounced, triggers server-side `ilike`)
- DM Account select: All / Closer / SDR
- Bucket select: All / A / B / C
- Bump Status select: All / No bumps / 1-2 bumps / 3+ bumps

### Outreach table — `renderOutreachTab()`

Columns: Checkbox | # | Name | Bucket | DM Account | Bumps (dots) | Temp | TG Handle | Source | Owner | Created | Last Bump | Actions

- Checkbox pattern: reuse hover-to-reveal pattern from `app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx` (show row number normally, checkbox on hover, always show when checked)
- Row click opens existing slide-over
- Per-row dropdown: Record Bump, Move to Warm, Edit, Delete
- No DnD (not needed for outreach list)
- Bump dots: same 4-dot pattern as current Pipeline table's cold_dm section

### Bulk action toolbar

Rendered above the table when `selectedOutreach.length > 0`:
- "{N} selected" label + Select All on Page / Deselect All buttons
- **Bump All** button — calls `bulkRecordBump(selectedIds)` with loading state
- **Move to Warm** button — calls `bulkUpdateStage(selectedIds, 'warm')`, refreshes both outreach and pipeline data
- **Delete** button (red) — confirmation dialog, then deletes
- **Cancel** button — clears selection

### Pagination

Reuse the KOL page's sliding-window pagination pattern (`app/kols/page.tsx` lines 3107-3161):
- "Showing X-Y of Z" text on left
- Previous / 5 numbered pages / Next on right
- Active page styled with `backgroundColor: '#3e8692'`
- 50 items per page
- Page changes trigger `fetchOutreach()` via useEffect

---

## Step 4: Update dashboard metrics

The `dashboardMetrics` `useMemo` currently counts `totalDmsSent` from the `opportunities` array. Since `getAll()` still fetches cold_dm opps, this continues to work. But add `outreachTotal` to the dashboard for accurate count when outreach data is loaded:

- "DMs Sent" metric: use `outreachTotal` (server count) when available, fall back to client count
- Add "Cold DMs" count to the Pipeline Summary row
